title: A Quiet Place: Day One Opens with Strong Global Debut
text: Paramount's prequel film achieved a franchise-record opening, earning an estimated $98.5 million globally during its debut weekend (June 28-30). The film opened with $53.0 million domestically in North America, surpassing both previous installments. According to the report, A Quiet Place: Day One began in 59 international markets, setting franchise opening records in 24 of them. International markets contributed $45.5 million, with China leading at $9.8 million, followed by Mexico ($4.7 million) and the UK/Ireland ($3.9 million). Director Michael Sarnoski took over from franchise creator John Krasinski, who served as screenwriter and producer. The sci-fi thriller stars Lupita Nyong'o, Joseph Quinn, and Alex Wolff, depicting an alien invasion of New York City. Premium formats performed well: IMAX screens generated $9.3 million globally, representing 9% of the total weekend gross. Eight additional markets were scheduled to open the film the following week, including Argentina, Chile, and Finland.
